snippet.title: Wings - Mull Of Kintyre
snippet.description:
Music video by Wings performing Mull of Kintyre, remastered in HD.

Directed by Michael Lindsay-Hogg

Mull of Kintyre features in 'The 7” Singles Box', a collection of 80 career-spanning 7” singles personally curated by Paul McCartney. The box set includes 163 tracks totalling over 10 hours of music, a 148-page book with a foreword by Paul, and is also released digitally.

'The 7” Singles Box' out 2 December 2022: https://PMC.lnk.to/7inSinglesBox

snippet.title: THE ROLLING STONES Opening SIXTY 2022 Berlin 08/03/22  last live concert Mick Jagger Keith Richards
snippet.description:
The Rolling Stones’ concert at the Waldbühne in Berlin on August 3, 2022, was remarkable for several reasons. It marked the end of their “Sixty-Tour,” possibly signifying their last major live performance. The venue, Waldbühne, is historically significant for the band, having hosted their infamous 1960s performance that led to legendary riots. This past gave the 2022 concert an added historical layer. The Waldbühne, an intimate setting for a world-renowned band, provided fans with an extraordinary closeness. Mick Jagger humorously interacted with the audience, expressing his love for Berlin by praising local specialties like Currywurst and “Berliner Luft.” The setlist included timeless hits such as “Paint It Black” and “(I Can’t Get No) Satisfaction,” enhancing the evening’s unique ambiance. Overall, this concert was not just a musical highlight but a piece of living history, emphasizing the band’s connection to Berlin and their fans.

🎸 #RollingStones #BerlinConcert #Waldbühne2022 #SixtyTourFinale #MickJagger #RockLegends #LiveMusicExperience #BerlinLove #Currywurst #BerlinerLuft #ClassicRock #PaintItBlack #Satisfaction 🎤🤘


🎸 #rollingstones #rock #therollingstones #mickjagger #keithrichards #music #rocknroll #rockandroll #charliewatts #thebeatles #blues #stones #classicrock #vintage #ronniewood #guitar #vinyl #vinylcollection #love #thestones #nirvana #art #rockmusic

snippet.title: Brave female customer tackles armed robber at east London shop #Shorts
snippet.description:
#Short #DRAMATIC Moment a brave customer tackles an armed would-be robber attempting to raid a convenience store in east London. 

The thug brandished what reassembled a knife at the shop assistant behind the counter, before the brave woman thwarted his attempts by restraining him. 

CCTV footage from the store shows the armed thug running from the store when he quickly realises his attack was futile.

If you're looking for a destination that's both exotic and beautiful, then look no further than Cameroon! This African country is home to some of the world's most stunning sights and sounds, and it's a great place to visit if you're looking for a unique travel experience.

In this video, we'll take you on a whirlwind tour of Cameroon, from the lush rainforest to the stunning coastline. We'll show you all the sights and sounds of this amazing country, and help you plan the perfect trip to Cameroon!

snippet.title: 25 MIN INTENSE ABS + CORE WORKOUT - No Equipment - Abs of Steel - No Repeat Home Workout
snippet.description:
💖 Find the right workout plan for you in my fitness app – let's grow together! https://quiz.growwithanna.com/
Team, are you ready to feel your Abs BURN? This INTENSE 25 MIN ABS + CORE Workout will do just that! No repeat ab exercises to help you activate your core, and with good nutrition - reduce belly fat! Focus on your form and don't quit - I know you can do it! 

▸ Focus: Core Strength
▸ Time: 25 Min
▸ Equipment: No Equipment, Bodyweight Only

Workout:
▸ 00:00 - 25:25 Workout 40 sec on, 10 sec off
Hollow Knee Hug Crunch
Bicycle Crunches
Deadbug + 3 sec hold
1 Arm Plank Left Arm
1 Arm Plank Right Arm
Reverse Crunches
Straight Leg Bicycles
Elbow to Knee - Hollow Crunch
Mountain Climbers
Plank Reach + Toe Touch Right
Plank Reach + Toe Touch Left
Leg Raise Hold
Flutter Kicks
Alternating Toe Tap Sit Up
V Sit Heel Taps
Low Plank Hold
Side Star Plank Right Side
Side Plank Dips Right Side
Side Star Plank Left Side
Side Plank Dips Left Side
Straight Leg Crunches
Leg Raise + Elbow to Knee Crunch
Russian Twists
2 Point Bear Plank
Low Plank Climbers
One Leg Hollow Crunch
Hollow Hold
Dolphin to Low Plank
Low Plank Side Steps
Low Plank Hip Dips

▸ 25:25 - 27:53 Cool Down 30 sec on, 10 sec off
Child’s Pose
Cobra Ab Stretch
Knee Hug

Please remember that we are all different and that you can make this your own workout ♡ Take a longer break when you need to.

snippet.title: Why Growth Mindset - Michael Jordan shares his story on Growth Mindset
snippet.description:
Why Should Having A Growth Mindset Matter To You? Read this 
https://www.36-hr.com/why-having-a-growth-mindset-matters-to-you/

Why Having A Growth Mindset Matters - 
In recent years, the term ‘Growth Mindset’, a term coined by Dr Carol Dweck, has become very popular and employers are now looking for leaders and employees with a strong growth mindset as part of their personal brand.  
Let’s look at what Michael Jordan, one of the greatest basketball of all times, has to share about Growth Mindset.
Michael Jordan made the famous decision: a career change. How could this great sports athelete, at the pinnacle of his success, walk away from glory and success? He also did the unthinkable. He risked everything.

To understand why he risked everything, let's take a peek into the mindset of Michael Jordan, the competitor:
"I can accept failure, everyone fails at something. But I can't accept not trying."  "I've always believed that if you put in the work, the results will come...I've failed over and over and over again in my life. And that is why I succeed."
"My attitude is that if you push me towards something that you think is a weakness, then I will turn that perceived weakness into a strength."
"If you're trying to achieve, there will be roadblocks. I've had them; everybody has had them. But obstacles don't have to stop you. If you run into a wall, don't turn around and give up. Figure out how to climb it, go through it, or work around it."
As Carol Dweck, author of Mindset, would say, Jordan is a prime example of the growth mindset. It's the mindset that almost every successful athlete who has had long-term success has. It says genetics may determine the starting line, but hard work determines the finish line. 
Failure isn't just accepted; it's expected. When you stretch yourself past your current limits, failure is inevitable. It spawns growth. You only reach the top and stay at the top by continually improving. Winning isn't everything. Growing is. The Result of Hard Work, Not Genetics
Michael Jordan wouldn't have risked everything if he honestly didn't believe hard work conquers all. MJ was not drunk. He didn't think just because he was successful at basketball that he'd be successful at baseball. Michael Jordan shares his growth mindset story: Hard work makes all the difference, and that’s the essence of Growth Mindset.  Fixed Mindset, on the other hand, believes in inborn talent and intellect.

WHAT IS A GROWTH MINDSET? WHY SHOULD I BOTHER ABOUT GROWTH MINDSET?
Your mindset is the sum of your thoughts and beliefs that determine how you make sense of yourself, your immediate environment, and the world.  It shapes how you would react and respond to all the events and changes in your life.  Dr Carol Dweck, a researcher and Professor of Psychology at Stanford University, says there are 2 types of mindsets: a growth mindset and a fixed mindset.
Those people with a growth mindset understand that knowledge can be acquired, intelligence and skills can be developed. With a growth mindset, people focus on improvement instead of worrying about how smart they are. They work hard to learn more and get smarter. A “growth mindset,” thrives on challenge and views failure not as evidence of unintelligence but as a great chance to learn and build upon existing skills and abilities.  They believe that learning doesn’t stop the moment you leave school or university. With time and effort, their intelligence and knowledge can grow because they recognize that their effort influences their success, so they spend more time practicing or learning, which leads to higher levels of achievement.  Adopting a growth mindset is not just essential in your life, it’s critical. 
On the other hand, a “fixed mindset” assumes that our character, intelligence, and creative ability are innate and cannot be changed.  People with a fixed mindset only believe that they have been successful when their performance or intelligence measures higher than the standard. They constantly strive for success and avoid failure because they fear other people will view them as being innately unintelligent. 

Having a fixed mindset leads to a belief that intelligence and talent are limited, so there is no use in working to develop or learn. And, because they believe they were born with a restriction on their talent, those with a fixed mindset put this same restriction on their effort– and subsequently, their success.  
To find out more about Growth Mindset workshop, contact 36 HR Training & Consultancy at http://www.36-hr.com.  Also get inspired by many Growth Mindset examples.

We also conduct customised Growth Mindset courses in Singapore, or Growth Mindset vs Fixed Mindset course for your high performance teams.  We share with you strategies on how to develop a growth mindset. Check out: 
https://www.36-hr.com/growth-mindset-workshop-singapore/

localized.title: Why Growth Mindset - Michael Jordan shares his story on Growth Mindset
localized.description:
Why Should Having A Growth Mindset Matter To You? Read this 
https://www.36-hr.com/why-having-a-growth-mindset-matters-to-you/

Why Having A Growth Mindset Matters - 
In recent years, the term ‘Growth Mindset’, a term coined by Dr Carol Dweck, has become very popular and employers are now looking for leaders and employees with a strong growth mindset as part of their personal brand.  
Let’s look at what Michael Jordan, one of the greatest basketball of all times, has to share about Growth Mindset.
Michael Jordan made the famous decision: a career change. How could this great sports athelete, at the pinnacle of his success, walk away from glory and success? He also did the unthinkable. He risked everything.

To understand why he risked everything, let's take a peek into the mindset of Michael Jordan, the competitor:
"I can accept failure, everyone fails at something. But I can't accept not trying."  "I've always believed that if you put in the work, the results will come...I've failed over and over and over again in my life. And that is why I succeed."
"My attitude is that if you push me towards something that you think is a weakness, then I will turn that perceived weakness into a strength."
"If you're trying to achieve, there will be roadblocks. I've had them; everybody has had them. But obstacles don't have to stop you. If you run into a wall, don't turn around and give up. Figure out how to climb it, go through it, or work around it."
As Carol Dweck, author of Mindset, would say, Jordan is a prime example of the growth mindset. It's the mindset that almost every successful athlete who has had long-term success has. It says genetics may determine the starting line, but hard work determines the finish line. 
Failure isn't just accepted; it's expected. When you stretch yourself past your current limits, failure is inevitable. It spawns growth. You only reach the top and stay at the top by continually improving. Winning isn't everything. Growing is. The Result of Hard Work, Not Genetics
Michael Jordan wouldn't have risked everything if he honestly didn't believe hard work conquers all. MJ was not drunk. He didn't think just because he was successful at basketball that he'd be successful at baseball. Michael Jordan shares his growth mindset story: Hard work makes all the difference, and that’s the essence of Growth Mindset.  Fixed Mindset, on the other hand, believes in inborn talent and intellect.

WHAT IS A GROWTH MINDSET? WHY SHOULD I BOTHER ABOUT GROWTH MINDSET?
Your mindset is the sum of your thoughts and beliefs that determine how you make sense of yourself, your immediate environment, and the world.  It shapes how you would react and respond to all the events and changes in your life.  Dr Carol Dweck, a researcher and Professor of Psychology at Stanford University, says there are 2 types of mindsets: a growth mindset and a fixed mindset.
Those people with a growth mindset understand that knowledge can be acquired, intelligence and skills can be developed. With a growth mindset, people focus on improvement instead of worrying about how smart they are. They work hard to learn more and get smarter. A “growth mindset,” thrives on challenge and views failure not as evidence of unintelligence but as a great chance to learn and build upon existing skills and abilities.  They believe that learning doesn’t stop the moment you leave school or university. With time and effort, their intelligence and knowledge can grow because they recognize that their effort influences their success, so they spend more time practicing or learning, which leads to higher levels of achievement.  Adopting a growth mindset is not just essential in your life, it’s critical. 
On the other hand, a “fixed mindset” assumes that our character, intelligence, and creative ability are innate and cannot be changed.  People with a fixed mindset only believe that they have been successful when their performance or intelligence measures higher than the standard. They constantly strive for success and avoid failure because they fear other people will view them as being innately unintelligent. 

Having a fixed mindset leads to a belief that intelligence and talent are limited, so there is no use in working to develop or learn. And, because they believe they were born with a restriction on their talent, those with a fixed mindset put this same restriction on their effort– and subsequently, their success.  
To find out more about Growth Mindset workshop, contact 36 HR Training & Consultancy at http://www.36-hr.com.  Also get inspired by many Growth Mindset examples.

We also conduct customised Growth Mindset courses in Singapore, or Growth Mindset vs Fixed Mindset course for your high performance teams.  We share with you strategies on how to develop a growth mindset. Check out: 
https://www.36-hr.com/growth-mindset-workshop-singapore/
